ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #28, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-28-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Gary Rothstein appeared by telephone for Union Bank (Trustee).
Liza Bunker appeared by telephone for Hanson Bridgett LLP.
Alicia Harris in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that a modest reduction in fees is in order to make sure it is a reasonable amount. Ms. Bunker stated that this accounting period involved some complex and volatile issues that they were facing and that advice from them was being requested and provided frequently. Ms. Harris stated that she does not have an Attorney and that she does not know if there is anything she is objecting to. Counsel stated that there was an eviction, sale of the property, and that things are settled down now and that the expenses should be somewhat lower going forward. The Petition for Order Settling Twenty-Eighth Account and Report of Trustee, etc., and the Petition for Allowance of Attorney's Fees and Costs are under submission.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Cabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #27, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Nov-16-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Liza Bunker for Union Bank (Trustee).

Ms. Bunker stated that she does not know when the Family is planning on going to Disneyland, that the trip has been postponed, that the Providers feel going is a good idea, that she believes they are looking for a new Doctor and that Lakisha's regular Doctor has retired. Ms. Bunker also stated that the Conservator feels that having the Grandmother go on the trip is required, that Lakisha requires 24-hour care, that the Grandmother is very emotionally involved and provides companionship, and that she has not received a stipend report or been given a timeline. In addition, Ms. Bunker stated that the bank requires multiple bids from Contractors, that she thinks only $30,000.00 in work is needed, that the bank has been looking at real estate, but has not settled on any house or location, and that they are working on staying within the limits to remain in the geographical area where Lakisha receives services. Furthermore, Ms. Bunker stated that some of the squatters were family members, that the family dynamic is very delicate, that the Bank is often in a position of addressing occupancy issues, that finding a new house with fewer bedrooms mitigates the issue of family members wanting to move themselves in, that storage is a necessity, that a single story home would be more practical, that the duplicate payments to Vivian and Alicia were an oversight, that there was a change in staff/caregivers overlapped, and that the bank does not want to recover the money from Alicia. The Court expressed that the duplicate payment ($6,750.00) should be eaten by the bank and stated that it was their responsibility. Ms. Bunker addressed attorney's fees making reference to the complexity of administration and multiple issues regarding eviction proceedings, spoke of family history, spoke of time spent on numerous issues that a professional Trustee might not have encountered, stated that a lot of work was done in making sure the family could stay together and that a 5% real estate commissions is acceptable. The Twenty Seventh Account and Report of Trustee, etc. is taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #27, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Oct-11-2017 Continued To Nov-08-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Sep-11-2017 Off Calendar

Twenty-Sixth Account And Report Of Trustee And Petition For Settlement Thereof, For Allowance Of Trustee'S Compensation And Fro Allowance Of Attorneys
Off Calendar Oct-22-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: William Hoehler for (trustee) MUFG Union Bank, N.A.

The Court stated that it is following the order on the 25th accounting regarding discussing the investment strategy as well as an issue with some fees, that at this point the income from the annuities is covering costs, that there is a premise of principal growth strategy, that it is not entirely conservative, that is raises questions such as are there other things that could enhance the quality of life, that this person is young and may depend on the trust for a long time, that she may need a greater level of care, that there could be significant medical costs and that it leads one to say why take risks. Mr. Hoehler reported that each year they try to figure out how else to spend the money on Ms. Wilson, that the unspent income is added to the principal, that there is not much more that can be done, that they are looking into hiring a medical advisor who may be able to find something else that can be done, that the principal continues to increase and that the expected future costs is the reason the trustee wants the principal invested. The Court stated that the investment strategy chosen is designed to do more than keep up with inflation, that greater risk is taken on at a greater rate, that the benefit is to the residual beneficiaries and that the court is not concerned with them. Mr. Hoehler stated that if the income became insufficient the trustee would need to decide what to do, that they already have excess of income and that they have decided to invest in equities and that the trustee wants to grow the principal. The Court stated that this is a court supervised trust for a specific person. Mr. Hoehler stated that the bank will do whatever the court orders and that they are currently using the capital appreciation level which is a mix of equity and fixed income. The Court asked if Ms. Wilson has an attorney or Guardian ad Litem, that it does not want to micro-manage, that the trustees are the experts, that it is concerned that the current strategies take on more risks than this court supervised trust allows, that the current risk level is not appropriate, that it is inclined to approve the 26th accounting with a directive to implement a balanced income approach and to look at this again next time to consider other concerns that the trustee has. Mr. Hoehler stated that with the courts direction the trustee will have no concerns in regards to the remainder beneficiary. The Court stated that 2 years from now they should plan to look at it again and decide if it is too conservative. Mr. Hoehler stated that in regards to the fee request, they relate to e-filing, that the accountings were carried forward under a civil case number, that they went back and forth with Ms. Hilton and it was decided that they needed a new case number, that this was a one time exercise and that whatever the court wants to do is fine. The Court asked Mr. Hoehler to submit a new order including the directive requested, to leave a blank for the attorney's fees and that the accounting petition will be taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Watts. (Not Reported)
